original_query: SELECT "NAME", "DDEPTNO", COUNT(*) AS "C" FROM (SELECT "NAME" AS "NAME", "DEPTNO" AS "DDEPTNO" FROM "DEPT") AS "t3" WHERE "NAME" = 'Charlie' GROUP BY GROUPING SETS(("NAME", "DDEPTNO"), "NAME")
rewritten_query:
SELECT "NAME", "DEPTNO" AS "DDEPTNO", COUNT(*) AS "C"
FROM "DEPT"
WHERE "NAME" = 'Charlie'
GROUP BY GROUPING SETS(("NAME", "DEPTNO"), "NAME");

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Removed unnecessary subquery that was only used for column aliasing. The aliasing can be done directly in the SELECT clause.
Simplified the query structure by eliminating the redundant subquery layer, which should allow the optimizer to work more efficiently.
